_doc: Honest inventory of every '### section' in the replay3 report.md and whether it is included in dedup_gold.json. Inclusion criterion: claim-bearing prose that parses into clean trailing-[N] claim units. Exclusion reasons: (a) no-claim chrome / broken OCR fragments (out of scope for DEDUP gold; that is the chrome fixture's job, iwire013/14); (b) claim-bearing but contains only distinct single-occurrence claims (keeps, no paraphrase_repeats) -> optional coverage, omitted to keep the fixture curated; (c) curated subset -> only the labeled cluster is in scope.

sections.reason: Clean narrative prose, all cite [1]; every unit a distinct claim (all keep). Provides negatives (no paraphrase_repeats).
Clean narrative prose, all cite [2]; every unit a distinct claim (all keep). Negatives.
Single curator-gap stub sentence ('did not survive strict verification'); no claim cluster to dedup.

CANONICAL POSITIVE. Two heavy paraphrase clusters (method 702/Gaussian; exposure measure = probability of computerisation) with IDENTICAL cites [8][9][10], plus a robot-density [4,5,6,7] cluster, plus distinct [4] claims and a varying-cite negative (unit 24 [4,5,6]).

sections.section: Generative-AI productivity field evidence (Brynjolfsson et al., QJE 2025)
sections.reason: Second clean positive: intervention / staggered-introduction / 5,172 agents restated ~7x, all cite [11].

sections.section: LLM occupational-exposure measurement (Eloundou et al., Science 2024)
sections.reason: Single curator-gap stub sentence; no claim cluster.
Broken OCR fragments (e.g. 'has not wiped out a majority of jobs extraction, synthesis of findings'); no-claim/garbled chrome -> out of scope (chrome fixture).
Fragmented recap of single claims plus garbled NYT-Book-Review fragment; not a clean paraphrase cluster -> out of scope.
Broken OCR/concatenated fragments (JEL classification, 'Governan; ce', 'b Polarization') -> chrome, out of scope.
Broken OCR fragment (truncated DOI mid-string, glued affiliation text) -> chrome, out of scope.
Broken OCR/concatenated fragment ('to associate people... [33](truncated)') -> chrome, out of scope.

372 units, mostly distinct single-occurrence claims and some chrome. ONLY the Stable Diffusion / text-to-image cluster (units 310-316) is in scope -- it is the report's only cluster exercising ORDER-INDEPENDENT citation-SET equality ([156,157,158] -> [157,156,158] -> [158,156,157]). The rest are out of scope (single-occurrence keeps add no discrimination).
Claim-bearing but a single distinct paragraph of limitations claims (no paraphrase_repeats) -> optional coverage, omitted to keep the fixture curated.
Claim-bearing but a single consolidated finding (one keep, no repeats) -> optional coverage, omitted.

Two section-final recap lines, both cite [1]. Cross-section duplicates of the Task-based section are OUT OF SCOPE (clustering is within-section only) -> each is a first within-section emission = keep. Negatives that guard against cross-section over-dedup.

scope_notes: Clustering is WITHIN-SECTION only; cross-section dedup is out of scope.
Two labels only: keep | paraphrase_repeat. No-claim chrome has no slot and is excluded entirely (chrome is the iwire013/14 chrome fixture's job).
paraphrase_repeat IFF: same claim already emitted earlier in the same section AND identical citation SET (order-independent) AND no new number/entity/fact. Otherwise keep. When unsure -> keep.
Varying-citation restatements are KEEP (negatives that catch a naive semantic dedup ignoring citations).
